Noun form converted to adjective; same semantic concept for tumor classification
Noun form converted to adjective; same semantic concept for tumor classification
Exact match found in full target enum
Obituary typically sourced from relatives/friends; weaker semantic link than direct sources
Same biospecimen source, different naming convention (anatomical location vs. sample type)
Synonym: feces is medical term, stool is common equivalent
Plasma is a blood component; more specific than general BLOOD category
Serum is a blood component; more specific than general BLOOD category
Pleural fluid is a specific bodily fluid; more specific than OTHER BODILY FLUIDS
Peritoneal fluid is a specific bodily fluid; more specific than OTHER BODILY FLUIDS
Same concept: imaging as evidence of recurrence; Phase 2 version includes specific imaging modalities
Initial diagnosis represents primary disease status
Progressive disease is one aspect of progression or recurrence
Recurrent disease is explicitly included in 'Progression or Recurrence' concept
Distant metastasis/recurrence refers to distant spread of disease
Loco-regional (local and regional) disease maps to regional disease status
Distant metastasis is a specific type of metastatic disease
Regional metastasis is a specific type of metastatic disease
NOS (Not Otherwise Specified) metastasis is equivalent to unspecified metastatic status
Absence of metastasis indicates confirmed non-metastatic status
Blood draw is the procedure; blood sample is the result - functionally equivalent in diagnostic context
Cytology is microscopic examination of cells, which is part of pathologic review process
Reformed smoker for extended period indicates former smoker status

subject_label: Current Reformed Smoker for < or = 15 yrs
comment: Reformed smoker with shorter duration still indicates former smoker status
Reformed smoker directly equivalent to former smoker
Undocumented history indicates information not reported
Missing duration information maps to unknown status
Cure and Curative are direct synonyms describing the same treatment intent
Cryoablation and radiofrequency ablation are both ablation modalities but distinct techniques
Different ablation methods; ethanol injection is distinct from radiofrequency
Microwave ablation and radiofrequency ablation are related thermal ablation techniques
Unspecified ablation type; radiofrequency ablation is most common specific form
Radiosurgical ablation is equivalent to stereotactic radiosurgery
Both ablation modalities but distinct techniques
Unspecified brachytherapy maps to general brachytherapy category
Chemoembolization combines chemotherapy and embolization; broader categorization under pharmacotherapy
Embolization is an interventional procedure related to surgery
Internal radiation is synonymous with brachytherapy
Combination radiation therapy may encompass concurrent approaches
CyberKnife is a stereotactic radiosurgery platform
External beam radiation direct terminology match
Radiation implants are a form of brachytherapy
Internal radiation is equivalent to brachytherapy
Proton beam radiation direct terminology match
Stereotactic radiosurgery and Gamma Knife are equivalent
Unspecified radiation therapy maps to general radiation therapy
Radioactive iodine is a radiopharmaceutical treatment
Stem cell treatment is a form of cellular therapy
Allogeneic stem cell transplant direct terminology match
Double autologous transplant is a variant of autologous stem cell transplantation
Haploidentical transplant is a type of allogeneic transplantation
Non-myeloablative transplant is a conditioning approach used in both autologous and allogeneic settings
Syngeneic transplant is genetically identical but distinct from standard allogeneic
Unspecified stem cell transplant maps to general stem cell transplant category
Surgery and surgical procedure are direct synonyms
Low grade source value directly matches G1 Low Grade target
Intermediate grade source value directly matches G2 Intermediate Grade target
High grade source value directly matches G3 High Grade target
Typo variant of the same concept - 'Chrimerism' is a misspelling of 'Chimerism' (coexistence of genetically distinct cell populations)
Both values indicate absence of gender identity information; 'unspecified' is semantically equivalent to 'Unknown' in this clinical context
